subject: First Aid & Safety
title: Blizzards and Whiteouts
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Blizzards and Whiteouts.

hook.text: In a whiteout, you can't tell the ground from the sky. People have walked in circles until they collapsed.

buildup.text: Blizzards combine heavy snow, strong winds over 35 mph, and low visibility below 1/4 mile.

discovery.text: Wind-driven snow packs into drifts that can bury cars, block roads, and collapse structures.

twist.text: Carbon monoxide from running a car in a snowdrift kills occupants within hours as exhaust accumulates.

climax.text: Staying in a sheltered vehicle with the window cracked is safer than walking into a whiteout.

punchline.text: In a blizzard, shelter wins. Walking into a whiteout is walking into nothing.

quiz.question: What hidden danger threatens people sheltering in cars during blizzards?
options: Carbon monoxide buildup from exhaust blocked by snow
The car running out of gas
